title: IMS Engineer
description: **About Telnyx** *Telnyx is an industry leader that's not just imagining the future of global connectivity—we're building it. From architecting and amplifying the reach of a* *private, global, multi\-cloud IP network* *, to bringing* *hyperlocal edge* *technology right to your fingertips through intuitive APIs, we're shaping a new era of seamless interconnection between people, devices, and applications.* *We're driven by a desire to transform and modernize what's antiquated, automate the manual, and solve real\-world problems through innovative connectivity solutions. As a testament to our success, we're proud to stand as a financially stable and profitable company. Our robust profitability allows us not only to invest in pioneering technologies but also to foster an environment of continuous learning and growth for our team.* *Our collective vision is a world where borderless connectivity fuels limitless innovation. By joining us, you can be part of laying the foundations for this interconnected future. We're currently seeking passionate individuals who are excited about the opportunity to contribute to an industry\-shaping company while growing their own skills and careers.* Telnyx is productizing a carrier\-grade IMS core on Kamailio. You’ll design, implement, and operate P\-/I\-/S\-CSCF functions at a global scale— **with VoLTE expertise and the ability to modify Kamailio’s C codebase when needed** . **What You’ll Do** * Design \& build: Own P\-/I\-/S\-CSCF call flows; configure Kamailio modules (ims\_\*, dispatcher, topos, tm, usrloc, permissions); integrate HSS/UDM/UDR. * Productize: Turn prototypes into supportable services with versioned configs, CI/CD, and observability. * Performance \& scale: Tune throughput, dialog/state handling, NAT traversal, DNS/SRV, TLS/mTLS; benchmark CPS/latency. * Reliability: Architect HA/DR; lead load tests, game days, incident reviews. * Security: Implement abuse/DoS controls (pike/htable/rate limiters), auth, encryption, and audit\-grade logging. * Upstream: Contribute fixes/patches and collaborate with the Kamailio community. * On\-call (shared): Keep the voice core healthy. **What You’ll Bring (must\-haves)** * VoLTE expertise: Hands\-on IR.92 (voice/SMS), registration/attach flows, SIP/SDP (AMR\-WB/EVS), QoS/bearers via PCRF/PCF, emergency calling, multi\-vendor interop. * Core IMS/SIP: 5\+ years with SIP/IMS; strong grasp of 3GPP (TS 23\.228, 24\.229, 29\.xx), CSCF roles, registration, service routing. * Kamailio mastery: Confident authoring kamailio.cfg routing logic; registrar/dialog/topology hiding; IMS modules in production. * Software engineering to modify Kamailio (required): * Proficient in C for high\-performance network services (memory management, concurrency, lock\-free/atomic patterns). * Experience building and changing Kamailio core/modules (parsers, transaction/dialog layers, ims\_\*), adding/adjusting AVPs/headers, and writing new module logic. * Tooling: gcc/clang, make, gdb, valgrind/AddressSanitizer, perf/flamegraph, packet tools (pcap, sngrep, ngrep). * Strong unit/functional testing habits; familiarity with Kamailio test approaches and CI. * Comfortable proposing and landing upstream PRs, engaging in code review, and maintaining forks/patch sets. * Systems \& SRE: Linux networking, DNS (NAPTR/SRV), TLS/mTLS, observability (Prometheus/Grafana, ELK/Loki/OpenTelemetry). * Automation: Git workflows, CI/CD, Docker/Kubernetes, IaC (Terraform/Helm), scripting (Python/Bash/Lua). * Communication: Clear design docs and incident write\-ups. **Nice to have** * FreeSWITCH/Asterisk; RTP/RTCP; media relays (rtpengine/rtpproxy); SBC concepts. * Diameter (Cx/Sh/Gx), ENUM/NP; VoWiFi/VoNR; SRVCC/eSRVCC exposure. * Prior upstream contributions to Kamailio or related SIP projects. * Telecom fraud mitigation and signaling security patterns.

Our data engineering initiatives represent one of our most critical technical focus areas, driving innovation across our global operations. **About the Role:** As a Junior Data Engineer, you will work on Citco's enterprise‑wide Centralized Data Platform (CDP) built on Databricks, contributing to our data infrastructure, API integrations, and future AI initiatives, while collaborating with data scientists, analysts, and business stakeholders to transform raw financial data into actionable insights; you will build and maintain data pipelines on the Databricks Lakehouse Platform using agile methodologies, develop scalable ETL/ELT processes, implement data quality controls, and ensure data governance standards are met, all within our AWS cloud environment to power critical business operations with the highest standards of data security and compliance. You'll be part of a team driving technical excellence and innovation within Citco's data engineering practice. **Key** **Responsibilities:** * Develop Databricks solutions leveraging Lakehouse architecture for enterprise data processing and analytics * Develop and optimize ETL/ELT pipelines * Utilize Delta Live Tables for data ingestion and transformations * Apply Unity Catalog features and IAM best practices for security governance and access control * Support infrastructure and resource management using Terraform * Participate in Agile/Scrum development process and collaborate with team members * Implement monitoring solutions for pipeline performance and data quality **Required Qualifications:** • 1\+ years of experience in data engineering • Experience with Python and familiarity with Spark programming • Demonstrable experience in using AI in development • Experience with AWS or other similar cloud services • Understanding of data modeling and SQL • Familiarity with the Databricks platform • Experience with version control systems (Git) • Strong problem\-solving and analytical skills • Excellent communication and collaboration abilities **Nice to Have:** • Financial services industry experience • Proven ability to learn and apply new technology **What Sets You Apart:** • Active interest in emerging technologies and industry trends • Experience implementing complex data solutions • Track record of continuous learning and skill development • Strong technical documentation abilities • Experience using AI tools to enhance productivity **Technical Environment:** • Primary Platform: Databricks • Cloud Platform: AWS (S3, Glue, Lambda) • Languages: Python, SQL • Tools: Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Git • Additional: Real\-time processing, API integrations **Our Benefits:** Your well\-being is of paramount importance to us, and central to our success. We provide a range of benefits, training and education support, and flexible working arrangements to help you achieve success in your career while balancing personal needs. Ask us about specific benefits in your location. We embrace diversity, prioritizing the hiring of people from diverse backgrounds. Our inclusive culture is a source of pride and strength, fostering innovation and mutual respect. Citco welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodation is available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection.

title: Junior Ecologist
description: The Junior Ecologist supports the delivery of ecological surveys, assessments, and reporting across a wide range of infrastructure, energy, renewables, and development projects. The role provides an opportunity to develop core consultancy skills while working as part of multidisciplinary project teams under the guidance of senior and principal ecologists. **Key Responsibilities** **Field Survey Support** * Assist with ecological field surveys including habitat surveys, protected species surveys, and monitoring work (e.g. GCN, bats, birds, otter, water vole – depending on experience and season). * Support data collection, recording, and interpretation in line with recognised survey guidance. * Follow health, safety, and biosecurity procedures during fieldwork. **Assessment \& Reporting** * Support the preparation of technical reports, including: + Ecological Impact Assessments (EcIA) + EIA ecology chapters + Baseline reports and survey summaries * Assist with data analysis, mapping, and presentation of findings. * Contribute to mitigation measures and recommendations under supervision. **Project Support** * Work within project teams to deliver ecology inputs to agreed scope, programme, and budget. * Assist with project administration, scheduling surveys, and coordination with colleagues. * Support senior staff in responding to client queries and regulator requests. **Learning \& Development** * Develop technical knowledge of UK wildlife legislation, planning policy, and best‑practice guidance. * Participate in training, mentoring, and technical development activities. * Build competency towards independent survey and reporting capability. **Collaboration \& Culture** * Work collaboratively with ecologists, EIA practitioners, planners, and other specialists. * Contribute positively to team culture and knowledge sharing. * Uphold ERM values, quality standards, and commitment to sustainability. **Essential** **Skills \& Experience** * Degree in Ecology, Environmental Science, or a related discipline. * Basic understanding of UK ecology, habitats, and wildlife legislation. * Willingness to undertake fieldwork, including early starts and occasional travel. * Strong attention to detail and good written communication skills. * Ability to work effectively as part of a team. * Full UK driving licence (or working towards). **Desirable** * Some experience of ecological field surveys (e.g. through placements, volunteering, or previous roles). * Familiarity with ecological survey methods and reporting. * Interest in developing specialist skills (e.g. ornithology, freshwater ecology, protected species). * Membership (or working towards membership) of a relevant professional body (e.g. CIEEM). * Basic GIS or data handling skills. **Behaviours \& Attributes** * Curious and keen to learn. * Reliable, organised, and proactive. * Positive attitude to feedback and development. * Commitment to high standards of safety and quality. * Passion for ecology and environmental protection. **Reporting \& Support** * Reports to: Senior Ecologist / Principal Ecologist / Ecology Team Lead * Works closely with: CPD project teams, EIA practitioners, and other technical disciplines **Why Join ERM as a Junior Ecologist** This role offers structured development within a leading global environmental consultancy, exposure to nationally significant projects, and a clear pathway for progression to Consultant and Senior Ecologist roles. Candidates may be invited to complete an online assessment as part of our recruitment process. All personal information will be handled confidentially and in compliance with data protection laws. ⠀ ERM is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to thrive, it’s an essential part of what makes ERM a great place to build a career and helps us create better solutions for our clients. We welcome talent from all backgrounds and provide equal opportunities for every candidate. If you have a disability, are neurodivergent, or need accommodations during the selection process, we’re here to support you. Our commitment doesn’t stop at hiring. Once you join us, we’ll ensure you have the tools, support, and adjustments needed to succeed and feel a true sense of belonging. Learn more about our Diversity, Inclusion \& Belonging (DIB) efforts by visiting our website or exploring our 2025 Sustainability Report..

title: Junior Sous Chef
description: **The Mespil Hotel has an exciting opportunity for a** **Junior Sous Chef** **to join our Kitchen** team. The main responsibilities of this role is to support the Head Chef and Sous Chef in the day\-to\-day operation of the kitchen, ensuring high standards of food quality, hygiene, and presentation are consistently delivered. The Junior Sous Chef plays a key role in supervising kitchen staff and maintaining a smooth and efficient service. This is a challenging position for a highly motivated, hardworking individual interested in developing themselves in the hospitality industry. Areas of responsibility will focus on the following areas: **Kitchen Operations:** * Assist in the preparation, cooking, and presentation of dishes to the highest standard * Support the Sous Chef and Head Chef in managing kitchen operations during service * Supervise and guide Commis Chefs and Chef de Parties * Ensure all food is prepared in line with recipes, portion control, and presentation standards * Maintain a clean, organised, and safe kitchen environment in line with HACCP standards * Monitor stock levels and assist with ordering and inventory control * Minimise food waste and support cost control initiatives * Ensure compliance with all food safety, health \& safety, and hygiene regulations * Assist in training and development of junior kitchen team members * Step in to lead a section or service in the absence of senior chefs * Assist with stock control, ordering, and inventory management * Minimise waste and ensure efficient use of ingredients * Work efficiently in a fast\-paced environment * Maintain a professional appearance and attitude * Adhere to all company policies and procedures ### **Candidate Requirements** ### **Key Requirements:** * Previous experience as a Chef de Partie or similar role in a busy kitchen * Strong knowledge of food preparation, cooking techniques, and kitchen operations * HACCP and food safety certification * Ability to work under pressure in a fast\-paced environment and meet deadlines * Strong organisational and time management skills * Team player with good communication and leadership ability * Passion for food, quality, and continuous improvement ### **Desirable:** * Formal culinary qualification * Experience in hotel or similar kitchen environments * Knowledge of menu development and cost control ### **Key Competencies:** * Attention to detail * Leadership and teamwork * Flexibility and adaptability * Commitment to quality and standards * Strong work ethic and reliability * Good communication and teamwork skills ### **Personal Attributes** * Passion for food and hospitality * Positive, can\-do attitude * Reliable, punctual, and professional * Willingness to learn and develop ### **Working Conditions:** * Shift work including evenings, weekends, and public holidays * Physically demanding role requiring long periods of standing **The benefits of working at the Mespil Hotel:** * Competitive Salary * Continuous training and professional development programs. * Complimentary stays at our sister property. * Additional days off based on your length of service. * Access to our Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) for you and your family. * Option to join our Pension Scheme after 1 year of service. * Option to avail of Health Insurance at reduced rates. * Complimentary buffet\-style meals in the staff canteen for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. * A referral program\-refer a friend to join our team and receive a reward. * Participation in the Bike 2 Work Scheme and the Tax Saver Commuter Ticket Scheme. * Employee recognition initiatives to celebrate your achievements. * An exciting calendar of social events. * Being part of a supportive and dynamic team in a hotel recognized as an "Outstanding Employer" for 2023, 2024, 2025 and 2026 through the Failte Ireland Employer Excellence Program

title: Junior privacy counsel
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * Partner with Product, Engineering, and Security teams to embed privacy\-by\-design principles and translate regulatory requirements into practical technical solutions. * Identify privacy risks and opportunities early, helping shape compliant product features, workflows, and internal processes. * Manage privacy\-related legal work—using AI tools to accelerate drafting and initial review of DPAs, privacy policies, LIAs, and DPIAs, while applying your judgment to validate, adapt, and ensure compliance with global data\-protection regulations. * Conduct privacy audits and coordinate work with external consultants and legal advisors, ensuring alignment across business and technical teams. * Monitor evolving privacy regulations and case law, sharing clear guidance internally to support ongoing compliance and sound decision\-making. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

title: Legal PA, Litigation & Dispute Management - Dublin
description: We are Eversheds Sutherland, a global law firm, with more than 5,000 people across over 30 countries. We’re full\-service with deep niche and sector experience. Whatever challenge, wherever in the world, we’re equipped and ready to meet it. We live our values, we’re purposeful and purpose\-led. So although the world is fast\-moving and rapidly changing, we see it as a place where everyone can thrive. We’re ambitious for our clients, our communities – and for you. Whether you’re starting out on your career or well established, whether you’re a lawyer or in business services. If you’re looking for what’s next, we are too. **What to expect** As a Legal PA within our Practice Group Support team, your role is pivotal. You’ll provide confidential, professional and proactive support to a designated group of stakeholders. Confidence, effective communication and influential skills are essential. We value an assertive and forward\-thinking approach. Here’s what to expect: **Diverse responsibilities:** Our Legal PAs handle a wide range of tasks, supporting various aspects of our business. Empowerment and mentorship: You’ll lead by example, fostering ongoing personal and professional development among junior colleagues and peers. **Continuous learning:** We invest in your professional development through our development plans. We offer personalised mentoring and coaching, technical training and soft skills courses to boost motivation and success in your career path. **Culture of feedback:** Our culture thrives on continuous feedback that builds confidence, development and recognition of achievements. **Pro Bono, Community and Diversity \& Inclusion (D\&I) opportunities:** Beyond your daily tasks, we encourage you to contribute to Pro Bono, Community and D\&I initiatives. Make a difference by using your skills to benefit our communities. **Ambition for people, clients and communities:** Our ambition extends beyond business success. We are equally committed to the growth and well\-being of our people, clients and the broader community. **Staying ahead:** Collaborate closely with lawyers, take ownership of tasks and exceed business expectations, anticipate challenges, adapt to changing technologies. in the legal sector. For talented, ambitious individuals there's no better place to be working in the law industry. **Role and key responsibilities** **Stakeholder engagement:** Conduct regular one to one meetings with stakeholders to discuss actions, priorities, expectations and future planning. Attend team meetings to stay informed and contribute effectively. Act as a liaison point for both internal and external clients, draft clear and concise replies to incoming correspondence. Assist with client queries during stakeholder absences. Maintain a flexible approach and adopt hybrid working to accommodate stakeholder and client needs. **Adaptability and collaboration:** Demonstrate client service excellence by being responsive to stakeholder requests. Support the wider PA team, covering for team members during absences or when required to ensure continued resource. **Efficient mailbox, diary and travel management:** Proactively manage mailboxes and diaries including travel coordination. Anticipate future needs and take initiative to resolve conflicts. **Meeting organisation and coordination:** Draft agendas and prepare for internal and external meetings. Ensure necessary equipment and refreshments for both physical and virtual meetings using available technology and resources. **Workflow management and delegation:** Own the end\-to\-end process, be accountable for successful conclusion of all stakeholder requests. Delegate tasks effectively across dedicated support teams such as billing, finance, team assistants, document production to meet expectations and deadlines in line with the Scope of Service. **Document management:** Undertake urgent and short document work. Collaborate with the Document Production Team for larger documents utilising our dedicated software. **Matter management:** Manage the entire client and matter opening process including electronic filing, collaborating with stakeholders and other business support teams. **Financial administration and compliance:** Provide financial administrative support to stakeholders, coordinating with billing and finance teams, ensuring compliance with legal, firm\-wide and practice\-specific policies and procedures. **Pitch and presentation creation:** Collaborate with the document production team to create impactful pitches and PowerPoint presentations for stakeholders. **Marketing and business development:** Coordinate and organise events, create event material, digital working lists and communicate with attendees to ensure a seamless end to end process. **Championing process improvements:** Be an advocate for process changes within the organisation, influence decisions and be a part of the solution. **Efficient administration:** Manage electronic filing, records maintenance and expenses effectively using the firm's designated software. **Technology utilisation:** Leverage the firm’s technology as a key business tool for managing, updating, reporting and research. Create and maintain data sites for efficient collaboration. Seek opportunities to embrace technologies that improve our ways of working and drive productivity in day\-to\-day activities. **Skills and experience** We are looking for: * Experience in a similar environment: Experience working within a comparable professional setting, providing seamless adaptation to our busy environment. * Client service excellence: Strive to exceed client expectations by delivering outstanding service. Anticipate client needs and proactively address any issues. * Problem\-solving and initiative: Be a proactive problem solver. Anticipate challenges, take initiative and implement solutions efficiently. Aspire for excellence in all tasks. Show dedication and commitment to achieving strategic goals. * Effective communication: Demonstrate excellent communication skills when liaising with stakeholders and clients. Your ability to convey information clearly and professionally is essential. * Delegation and ownership: Delegate tasks across dedicated support teams while maintaining overall ownership of transactional activities. * High\-level support: Provide exceptional PA support to senior stakeholders, including managing calendars, organising meetings and handling confidential information. * Relationship building: Cultivate strong relationships both internally and externally. Collaborate effectively with colleagues and clients. * Task management and risk awareness: Skilfully manage tasks with expertise, seeking ways to enhance efficiency and quality. Balance priorities and stay alert to risks, ensuring tasks are completed on time and stakeholder expectations are met. * Flexibility: Demonstrate flexibility and embrace change. Adapt seamlessly to evolving work environments. * Microsoft applications proficiency: Demonstrate intermediate to advanced skills in Microsoft applications including Outlook, Teams and Copilot. * Team collaboration: Contribute to a positive team environment. Offer support to colleagues when needed. **Diversity \& Inclusion** At Eversheds Sutherland, “Inclusive” is a core business value. We bring together different skillsets, global mindsets and approaches. We foster diversity of thought and the freedom to put ideas into action. We have an inherent respect for the individual. We have a strong belief in collaboration and teamwork. Sharing ideas, asking questions, solving challenges and meeting our clients’ goals: together. We want all our people to thrive at work and reach their full potential and we work hard to continue to build a diverse and inclusive culture, monitor and report on our progress and impact, and develop our approach. This is reflected in our policies, systems and processes, and in our work with diversity membership organisations. Many of our people work flexibly in some way and we are open to considering how we can accommodate flexible working arrangements alongside role requirements. If this is important to you, please talk to us about it during the recruitment process. We want you to perform at your best during our recruitment process. If there is any adjustment or support you need, please contact us so we can discuss how we can best assist you. **For you,** **For your success.** **And what's next.**

title: Maintenance Contract Sales/Internal Sales
description: **Job Title** **Service \& Maintenance Contracts Sales Executive** (Construction / Automation \& Access Systems) **Department** Sales working with Service / Maintenance Team **Reports To** Sales Director *with strong day\-to\-day liaison with* Service \& Maintenance Manager **Role Summary** This role is responsible for growing and managing revenue from service and maintenance contracts across our installed base of automated gates/doors, barriers, blockers/bollards, folding gates, car parking systems, CCTV and access control. You will: * Convert newly completed installations/upgrades into maintenance contracts by proactively following up recent project customers. * Price and quote additions/extra equipment requested by customers on existing maintenance agreements. * Support the sales team by pricing maintenance contracts for new installation/upgrade quotations. * Generate your own leads and win new maintenance contracts from new and existing customers. The role is primarily office\-based, with regular site visits for customer meetings, surveys and onboarding/handover. **Location \& Travel** * Office\-based at: Dublin Office, Mulhuddart Dublin 15 * Regular site visits across: Mainly Leinster region but we do cover the full RoI * Travel options: use own vehicle (mileage reimbursed) or use company pool cars (subject to availability/policy) * Full driving licence required **Key Responsibilities** **1\) Follow\-up of Newly Completed Installations (Core)** * Track completed installations/upgrades and ensure every eligible customer is contacted within agreed timescales (e.g., within 2–4 weeks of handover). * Introduce and sell appropriate service packages (PPM, reactive cover, SLA tiers, parts/labour options). * Gather/confirm asset and site information needed to contract accurately (asset register, access arrangements, site rules, contacts, billing/PO requirements). * Coordinate internal handover into service delivery once contract is won. **2\) Existing Contract Growth – Pricing Additions \& Variations** * Price and quote additional equipment and contract variations requested by customers (e.g., added gates/doors, extra barriers, additional CCTV/access control doors, intercoms, safety device upgrades, extra call\-out cover, upgraded response times). * Ensure contract documentation is updated with revised scope, asset list, visit frequency and commercial terms as applicable. * Identify upsell opportunities during account conversations (additional sites, additional assets, upgrades to improve reliability/compliance). **3\) Support the Sales Team – Maintenance Pricing for New Works** * Work closely with the new works / projects sales team to price maintenance options alongside new installation/upgrade proposals. * Provide standardised service contract options that align with operational capacity and margin requirements. * Attend joint meetings/site surveys where needed to scope maintenance requirements early. * Ensure maintenance contract proposals clearly state scope, assumptions, exclusions, SLA and pricing. **4\) New Lead Generation – Win New Maintenance Contracts** * Proactively “hunt” for new opportunities through outbound calling, email campaigns, networking, referrals and targeting estates/FM organisations. * Build relationships with facilities managers, property management companies, main contractors, and end users to secure service contracts. * Maintain a healthy pipeline and deliver agreed targets for activity and revenue. **5\) Administration, CRM \& Commercial Governance** * Maintain accurate records in CRM: leads, opportunities, quotes, meetings, next actions and outcomes. * Provide forecasting and reporting (pipeline, conversion rates, order intake). * Ensure all proposals follow internal approval processes (pricing, margin, terms, credit checks where required). * Work closely with service scheduling, engineers, parts/admin and finance to ensure smooth onboarding and billing. **Products / Systems Covered** Automated gates and door automation, folding gates, barriers, blockers/bollards, car parking systems, CCTV, access control and associated safety devices and controls. **Skills \& Experience** **Essential** * Proven experience in B2B sales / account management (construction, service/maintenance, technical products or similar). * Confident in outbound calling and structured follow\-up of warm leads (recent installs). * Strong written communication (clear quotes/proposals) and numeracy (pricing, margin). * Highly organised: able to manage multiple customer follow\-ups, quotes and stakeholders. * Comfortable visiting sites and engaging customers in construction/FM environments. * CRM competence and good Microsoft Office skills. * Determination to succeed and strong interest in closing sales * Full driving licence. **Desirable** * Experience with service agreements/PPM/SLA sales. * Knowledge of automation/security systems (gates/doors/barriers/access control/CCTV) or similar electro\-mechanical systems. * Familiarity with construction/FM processes (inductions, permits, RAMS interfaces, contractor requirements). **Behaviours / competencies** * Proactive and persistent; strong follow\-through. * Customer\-focused, professional, and solution oriented. * Collaborative: works well with sales and service title: Medical Scientist - Haematology - HR26024
description: Job Details **Reference:** HR26024 **Job Nature:** Full Time Permanent **Job Location:** St Vincent's University Hospital **Grade:** Medical Scientist **Overview** Applications are invited for the position of Medical Scientist \- Haematology in the Pathology Department of St. Vincent’s University Hospital. Informal Enquiries or Role Specific Enquiries to: Ms Chrissann Fleming \- cfleming@svhg.ie (Chief of Haematology) Please download the below job description for this position, as this will not be available online after the vacancy close date. Applications for St. Vincent’s University Hospital job vacancies will only be accepted through SVUH Careers Page. Any applications submitted after the vacancy has closed will not be accepted. You will receive a confirmation email within 2 working days that your application has been received. We highly recommend that you contact us by emailing recruitment@svuh.ie if you do not hear from us. Closing date of applications: 01/05/2026 To be considered for this role you will be redirected to and must complete the application process on our careers page. To start the process, click the Apply button below to Login/Register. Key Responsibilities **Key Duties and Responsibilities:** **GENERAL** * Maintain throughout the Hospital awareness of the primacy of the patient in relation to all hospital activities. * Participate in developing the Pathology service to meet the clinical needs of our patients and ensuring that the service is consistent with the mission, vision values and strategic plan of the Hospital. * Participate in a team of skilled staff, creating and promoting healthy working relationships. * Participate in the implementation of operational processes to the standards of ‘best practice’, in order to optimise use of resources. * Participate in the implementation of a ‘quality management’ programme, which is patient centred and which measures and audits performance and client satisfaction. * Actively participate in and promote continuing education and research activities consistent with the values of the Hospital. * Participate in the concept of open communications throughout the Hospital. **SERVICE** * Ensure that all samples are processed in accordance with standard operating procedures. * Be responsible for the quality of their work and carry out their duties in accordance with Hospital policy. * Observe the strictest confidence when dealing with all aspects of patient or Hospital information. * Provision of appropriate interpretation on both analytical and interpretative aspects within the Haematology Laboratory. * Monitoring and ensuring the quality of analyses through active assessment of internal quality control and external quality assessment. * Taking an active part in training of junior medical scientists / student medical scientists and junior medical staff on Haematology related topics where appropriate. * Participation in the process of evaluation of new methods, techniques and equipment. * Participation in the continuance of clinical pathology accreditation and audit. * Be competent in aspects of Information Technology e.g. the use of APEX, Q\-Pulse and other MS systems as appropriate. * Participate in the annual joint review/personal development planning process with the Chief Medical Scientist * In co\-operation with the Consultant Head of Department, Chief Medical Scientist and other designated senior staff, participate in the introduction of new ideas and methods according to hospital policy. * Co\-operate in evaluating assays, equipment, consumable items and research projects where required. * Participate in the Major Emergency Plan for isolated incidents or multiple trauma according to Laboratory Management Team policy. * Participate with the Chief Medical Scientist in facilitating the training of students from relevant college courses. * Participation in research and development projects when required. * Participation in appropriate committees as required. * Actively participate in internal and external quality control and quality assurance programmes. **HEALTH AND SAFETY** * Participate in ensuring that effective safety procedures are in place to comply not only with the Safety, Health and Welfare at Work Act but also within the spirit of the Hospital’s mission, vision and values, and that they are known and followed. * Assist and co\-operate with the Laboratory Management Team in developing procedures to be followed in preventing and managing accidents occurring in the Laboratory area. Our Requirements Please refer to the job description or candidate information booklet attached to this advertisement. What We Offer Please refer to the job description or candidate information booklet attached to this advertisement. Candidate Information Booklet This booklet contains all the information candidates need to know about the role. View PDF

title: Mid Full Stack Developer (Python / React / AWS)
description: **Full Stack Developer (Python / React / AWS)** Salary: €60,000 to €80,000 DOE \+ bonus \+ benefits Location: Dublin City Centre Hybrid: 3 days per week in the office Our client is a global financial services company supporting investment funds, multinational companies and private clients worldwide. With over 6,000 employees across more than 40 countries, they build technology platforms that support key financial operations such as treasury, collateral management and trade processing. Dublin is one of their main technology hubs where many strategic systems are developed. Our client is looking for a Full Stack Developer to join a global engineering team building core platforms used by treasury, collateral and trade operations teams. You will work across the full development lifecycle building modern cloud based systems. The stack is focused on React on the frontend and Python on the backend running on AWS. There is currently a strong focus on improving and redesigning user interfaces across multiple applications. **Must Have (Key Requirements)** * 2\+ years Python development experience * AWS cloud experience * Experience using code generation tools such as GitHub Copilot or Amazon Q * Experience with a JavaScript framework such as React or Angular * Experience building or integrating APIs * Strong understanding of system design and modern development practices * The main stack includes Python, React, FastAPI, PostgreSQL and AWS. Experience with Django or Flask is also relevant. **Nice to Have** * DevOps experience * Cloud native architecture experience * Understanding of GenAI or agentic systems * Event driven architecture * Financial services experience **What You’ll Do** * Build new features and improve existing platforms * Develop frontend interfaces and backend services * Design and integrate APIs * Work with AWS cloud services and databases * Write unit tests and support test driven development The hiring manager is looking for **strong engineers who enjoy building systems and working across both frontend and backend** in a collaborative team environment.

title: Operations Administrator - Life Insurance
description: We are currently looking for an **Operations Administrator** to join our Client’s Services \& Administration Team. The place of work is **Dublin, Ireland.** This is an essential role focused on daily business, business transactions and client relationships. You will be responsible for executing the day\-to\-day operational requirements of the business. The operating environment we work in is ever\-changing and we strive to provide the highest level of service to our clients and partners. A key part of this role is adapting to change and continuously looking to improve the way we do things. The role will suit a highly motivated individual interested in working in a challenging and changing environment. **Main Responsibilities** • Process any type of transaction at policy level (New Business, Top Up, Switch, Partial Surrender, Total Surrender, Death Claim). • Manage day\-to\-day BAU requests coming from the markets. • Liaise with Asset Managers, Custodian Banks, and Intermediaries to ensure client expectations are met. • Read and prepare Custodian Banks and Asset Managers’ documents. • Produce and send disinvestment/investment/transfer orders to Custodians and Asset Managers. • Execute payment orders because of surrender requests. • Produce and send policy letters to clients. • Data entry and data quality review. • Assist in project implementation, mostly user acceptance testing (UAT). • Work efficiently and be able to prioritise workload within a fast\-paced environment. • Adhere to strict SLAs. • Promote a qualitative approach at all times. • Promote a culture of continuous improvement in daily operations. **Professional Skills** • Provide a high level of professional and operational behaviour. • Self\-motivated and able to work on own initiative. • Problem\-solving and decision\-making skills. • Highly resilient and adaptable to change within a fast\-paced environment. • Understand business need for flexibility and modify behaviour accordingly. • Strong communication skills in all formats (verbal, written) and ability to effectively communicate with team. • Social \& self\-awareness: interact with others in a socially acceptable manner. • Excellent organizational skills. • Attention to detail is crucial. **Qualifications \& Technical Skills** • \+2 years’ experience in Operations of Financial Institutions, Brokerage, or Life Insurance (desirable). • Fluent in English; other languages are an advantage. • Strong knowledge of Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. • Ability to manage multiple stakeholders, including clients, intermediaries, and banks. • Experience with policy administration systems. • Adherence to processes with high accuracy. • Cross\-border operations experience, including multi\-currency transactions and international client servicing. This is a unique opportunity to join a passionate, ambitious, and truly international team of professionals with vast experience in the insurance and financial sector. NM: 178565

title: Packaging Operative
description: **Packaging Operative \- Avoca Central Bakery \& Kitchen Bray** Avoca operates in 13 locations across the country. After all this time, the ethos of Avoca remains the same. We cherish our time\-honoured traditions, which have been lovingly passed down through several generations. Our skills might be steeped in a long and rich tradition, but our attitude is to look to the future. We are now heralded as one of Ireland's most exciting retail stores, with a host of award\-winning cafes, restaurants and food markets crammed with artisanal ingredients from near and far. **Job Summary:** The Packing Operative will be responsible for the day\-to\-day function of the CBK packaging activity. This role is a highly focused on food packaging. A Food Packaging Operative prepares, packs, and labels food products on production, ensuring quality standards and safety regulations (HACCP) are met. Responsibilities include operating machinery, inspecting products for defects, maintaining workstation hygiene, and working efficiently in fast\-paced, between ambient and chilled environments. Requires attention to detail, teamwork, and manual dexterity. **Responsibilities:** * Efficiently pack, seal, and label food products, often at high speeds. * Inspect items for defects, damage, or contamination before packaging. * Set up, operate, and make minor adjustments to packing machinery. * Adhere to strict health, safety, and hygiene standards (PPE required, such as hairnets, bear snood, gloves, coats, safety shoes). * Keep work areas and machinery clean and sanitized. * Complete daily paperwork, including packaging and labelling records. * Move packed goods to racks, dispatch crates or storage areas. **Requirements:** * Attention to Detail. Ability to spot quality issues and follow exact specifications. * Ability to stand for long periods and perform repetitive manual tasks. * Work effectively with colleagues and report faults to supervisors. * Basic skills for reading instructions, labelling, and checking quantities. * Strong work ethic with a focus on punctuality and attendance. * Requires no formal experience (entry\-level), with on\-the\-job training provided. * Previous experience in manufacturing, food, or warehouse roles is advantageous Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment to meet deadlines

title: Process Technician Apprentice
description: **Process Technician Apprenticeship** ***Are you looking to kick start a career as a Process Technician and get an education while getting paid?!*** If so, Pfizer Grange Castle has an apprenticeship opportunity within a state\-of\-the\-art Biopharmaceutical manufacturing plant for you! *In partnership with the Irish MedTech Association \& IBEC our Engineering network within Pfizer Grange Castle we are seeking candidates for a Process* *Technician Apprenticeship.* *This is a 2\-year program combining part time learning and work placement, leading to attainment of Higher Cert (Level 6\) in Bio Process and Chemical Process Technician.* This programme includes both technical and academic content with a key focus on manufacturing. This is an opportunity to join a world class facility who is dedicated to bringing therapies that will significantly improve patients’ lives. We are looking for an **Apprentice Process Technician** to join our team, this role will support manufacturing operations by assisting with routine production activities, including equipment preparation, material handling, in‑process checks, basic equipment verification. The role includes supporting sampling activities, preparation of materials or solutions for manufacturing use, stock control, and maintenance of production equipment and tools. This role is an on the job, off the job learning. **You will be required to attend college for 15 weeks block release at TU Dublin** **You will return to Pfizer for 9 months. When onsite in Pfizer Grange Castle, you will work on a Shift Pattern which may include Days \& Nights. This will be discussed in advance!** **About the Role and Responsibilities:** You will be directly involved in the manufacturing of our products, ensuring that they meet all specifications for safety, potency, and efficacy. You will work within the guidelines of current Good Manufacturing Practices (cGMP) to ensure conformance with all regulatory requirements and site Standard Operation Procedures (SOP’s), while assisting in the improvement of all current processes through our Continuous Improvement Program. * Assist in maintaining a safety orientated culture, cGMP compliant work environment always. * Perform initial troubleshooting of issues identified during routing operations. * Assist with the investigation of operational deviations through Quality System, engaging with relevant functions as appropriate. * Provide input into the creation and maintenance of area SOPs and batch records. * Preparation of materials for batches and batch manufacture of bio pharmaceutical products. * Use Lean tools as part of daily operations (e.g., Method 1, 5S) to optimise efficiency and drive culture of Continuous Improvement and Zero Defects. * Assist in facility start up, installation and validation activities to support the introduction of new products * This is a shift\-based role (For 24/7 Operations), Day and Night shifts. **Candidate requirements:** * Minimum Leaving Certificate. * Have a passion for Science and/or Technology, Engineering. * Demonstrate a strong work ethic. * Work well as part of a team. * Self\-motivated / self\-starter * Excellence in systems and practical applications. * Have good time management \& prioritisation skills * Be committed to excellence in operational and compliance requirements * Focused on process improvements within a structured environment. * Willingness to learn and grow. * Willing to do Shift Work. * Willing to start September 2026 **Additional Information** * In order to be considered for this position in Ireland you need to be legally eligible to work in Ireland. * Please note there is no relocation support available for this position **How To Apply** * Make a difference today, all suitable candidates should apply with CV below. We are looking forward to hearing from you! Purpose **Breakthroughs that change patients' lives** ... At Pfizer we are a patient centric company, guided by our four values: courage, joy, equity and excellence. Our breakthrough culture lends itself to our dedication to transforming millions of lives. Digital Transformation Strategy One bold way we are achieving our purpose is through our company wide digital transformation strategy. We are leading the way in adopting new data, modelling and automated solutions to further digitize and accelerate drug discovery and development with the aim of enhancing health outcomes and the patient experience. Equal Employment Opportunity We believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce is crucial to building a successful business. As an employer, Pfizer is committed to celebrating this, in all its forms – allowing for us to be as diverse as the patients and communities we serve. Together, we continue to build a culture that encourages, supports and empowers our employees. Disability Inclusion Our mission is unleashing the power of all our people and we are proud to be a disability inclusive employer, ensuring equal employment opportunities for all candidates. We encourage you to put your best self forward with the knowledge and trust that we will make any reasonable adjustments to support your application and future career. Your journey with Pfizer starts here! Support Services

title: Product Analyst
description: **Base Location:** You'll be expected to spend 50% of your working week in one of the following locations : Dublin, Reading, Havant, Inverness, Aberdeen, Glasgow and Perth **Salary:** €49,400 \- €74,200 performance\-related bonus and a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Salary:** £49,004 \- £57,728 and and a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Working Pattern:** Permanent \| Flexible First options available **Role Overview** As an experienced AI Product Analyst, you will join SSE’s IT Product Analysis team to lead analysis across our AI domain and support the delivery of high profile, customer facing initiatives. You’ll play a key role in driving SSE’s largescale transformation by shaping AI driven products, ensuring strong data protection practices, and guiding teams toward the highest impact opportunities. A core part of this role is staying ahead of the rapidly evolving AI landscape. You are genuinely passionate about AI, continually learning about emerging tools and technologies, and monitoring market and competitor activity to help shape our strategy. **You Will** * Lead analysis across AI and IT initiatives, delivering high\-quality product insights, requirements, documentation, and clear as\-is / to\-be process definitions. * Drive business analysis work across multiple projects, ensuring delivery within expected time, cost, and quality constraints. * Support creation of Power BI dashboards and reporting to provide performance insights and inform decision\-making. * Produce Data Protection and Compliance documentation, identifying and assessing regulatory, data, and technical risks across AI and IT initiatives. * Monitor AI market trends, competitor capabilities, and emerging tools while collaborating with Product Managers, Engineering, Data Science, and Business teams to prioritise and deliver the highest\-value work. **You Have** * Strong understanding of AI/ML fundamentals, responsible AI principles, and the ability to translate model outputs into actionable insights. Skilled in analytics, Power BI (DAX, data modelling) and Databricks. * Solid knowledge of GDPR, privacy by design, data governance, and secure data lifecycle management, ensuring all AI and analytics initiatives meet regulatory and internal compliance standards. * Experienced in writing user stories, defining requirements, documenting as\-is/to\-be processes, applying design thinking, and prioritising high impact work. Skilled in applying Agile frameworks such as Scrum and Kanban, and proficient with Azure DevOps (ADO) and modern product delivery practices. * Broad IT foundation with exposure to data pipelines, cloud platforms, system integrations, and modern application architecture * Genuine passion for AI with a clear plan to stay up to date. Actively tracks new AI tools, market trends, and competitor activity to influence forward\-looking strategy. **About SSE** SSE has a bold ambition – to be a leading energy company in a net zero world. We're investing around £10 million a day in homegrown energy to help power a cleaner, more secure future. Our investment will see us build the world's largest offshore wind farm and transform the grid to deliver greener electricity to millions. Our IT division powers growth across all SSE business areas by making sure we have the systems, software and security needed to take the lead in a low carbon world. They provide expertise, advice and day\-to\-day support in emerging technologies, data and analytics, cyber security and more. **Flexible Benefits To Fit Your Life** Enjoy discounts on private healthcare and gym memberships. Wellbeing benefits like a free online GP and 24/7 counselling service. Interest\-free loans on tech and transport season tickets, or a new bike with our Cycle to Work scheme. As well as generous family entitlements such as maternity and adoption pay, and paternity leave. **Work with an equal opportunity employer** SSE will make any reasonable adjustments you need to ensure that your application and experience with us is positive. Please contact DAvid.brickell@sse.com / 01738 275 846 to discuss how we can support you. We're dedicated to fostering an open and inclusive workplace where people from all backgrounds can thrive. We create equal opportunities for everyone to succeed and especially welcome applications from those who may not be well represented in our workforce or industry. **Ready to apply?** Start your online application using the Apply Now box on this page. We only accept applications made online. We'll be in touch after the closing date to let you know if we'll be taking your application further. If you're offered a role with SSE, you'll need to complete a criminality check and a credit check before you start work.

title: Project Officer - Health Information and Standards / Oifigeach Tionscadail- Faisnéis agus Caighdeáin Sláinte
company: HIQA - Health Information and Quality Authority
description: **Project Officer \- Health Information and Standards** **Location:** Dublin, Cork or Galway **Grade:** Engineer Grade III **Tenure:** 2 x Permanent Role, 1 x Fixed Term Role until 28th July 2027 HIQA has a role to promote safety and quality in the provision of health and social care services for the benefit of the health and welfare of the public. There are five different functions within the Health Information and Standards Directorate which are outlined below. These roles will work within the National Care Experience Programme (NCEP). NCEP carry out national service\-user experience surveys and engagements across a range of Health services, in conjunction with the Department of Health, the Mental Health Commission and the HSE. Home \- National Care Experience Programme. There are a number of roles available within the NCEP, they will either work in the operational or the data and digital stream. The core purpose is to support the team to meet its operational or data management commitments. It includes project management, communication, administration, supporting the team to meet best practice in relation to data management requirements, and all roles involve extensive stakeholder engagement. Project Officers play a key role in supporting the operational or data management stream within the NCEP function, ensuring tasks are achieved in a timely and professional manner. **Essential:** * Degree qualification (minimum of Level 8 on the National Framework of Qualifications or equivalent) in a professional health and social care qualification, health related science, public health, business management, operations, or an area deemed relevant to the role by the Authority **and** * A minimum of 12 months’ post\-graduate experience in the following areas (note: work conducted as part\-fulfilment of an academic award at degree or masters level should not be included here): * Practical experience in project management and operational support * Experience with data management, digital design or working with communication teams * Experience in writing, reviewing, editing and proofing documents and reports for a variety of audiences. **Benefits of working at HIQA:** * Permanent position * 25 days annual leave * Pension Scheme * Learning and Development Opportunities * Well\-being Programme * Social Club * Employee Assistance Programme * Academic Education Support Scheme. Full details and application form at Careers with HIQA (recruitisland.ie). The completed application form must be submitted **by 5:00pm Monday 18th May 2026** . Only web applications fully completed on Careers with HIQA (recruitisland.ie) will be accepted. CVs will not be accepted. Dé Luain, an 18 Bealtaine 2026** . Ní ghlacfar ach le hiarratais ghréasáin atá comhlánaithe go hiomlán ar Careers with HIQA (recruitisland.ie). Ní ghlacfar le CVanna. title: Project Officer - Health Information and Standards / Oifigeach Tionscadail- Faisnéis agus Caighdeáin Sláinte
description: HIQA is an independent authority established to drive high\-quality and safe care for people using our health and social care services in Ireland. Our role is to develop standards, inspect and review health and social care services and support informed decisions on how services are delivered. If you are focused, motivated and seeking a change in your career, then have a look at our current vacancies. The Health Information and Quality Authority is an equal opportunities employer. **Project Officer \- Health Information and Standards** **Location:** Dublin, Cork or Galway **Grade:** Engineer Grade III **Tenure:** 2 x Permanent Role, 1 x Fixed Term Role until 28th July 2027 HIQA has a role to promote safety and quality in the provision of health and social care services for the benefit of the health and welfare of the public. There are five different functions within the Health Information and Standards Directorate which are outlined below. These roles will work within the National Care Experience Programme (NCEP). NCEP carry out national service\-user experience surveys and engagements across a range of Health services, in conjunction with the Department of Health, the Mental Health Commission and the HSE. Home \- National Care Experience Programme. There are a number of roles available within the NCEP, they will either work in the operational or the data and digital stream. The core purpose is to support the team to meet its operational or data management commitments. It includes project management, communication, administration, supporting the team to meet best practice in relation to data management requirements, and all roles involve extensive stakeholder engagement. Project Officers play a key role in supporting the operational or data management stream within the NCEP function, ensuring tasks are achieved in a timely and professional manner. **Essential:** * Degree qualification (minimum of Level 8 on the National Framework of Qualifications or equivalent) in a professional health and social care qualification, health related science, public health, business management, operations, or an area deemed relevant to the role by the Authority **and** * A minimum of 12 months’ post\-graduate experience in the following areas (note: work conducted as part\-fulfilment of an academic award at degree or masters level should not be included here): + Practical experience in project management and operational support + Experience with data management, digital design or working with communication teams + Experience in writing, reviewing, editing and proofing documents and reports for a variety of audiences. **Benefits of working at HIQA:** * Permanent position * 25 days annual leave * Pension Scheme * Learning and Development Opportunities * Well\-being Programme * Social Club * Employee Assistance Programme * Academic Education Support Scheme. title: Project Scheduler
description: **Project Scheduler \- Data centres** **Location: Dublin** **Fulltime/Permanent** **Salary: €60,000 \- €85,000** \*\*\*Junior and Senior roles available\*\*\* **Company Overview** A leading construction and engineering organisation delivering complex projects across high\-value sectors including mission\-critical and data centre environments. The business is built on a culture of continuous improvement, collaboration, and innovation, with a strong focus on embracing new technologies and driving best practice across project delivery. **Key Responsibilities:** * Prepare contract and project programmes, including ongoing monthly progress reporting * Monitor project progress, outputs, trends, and resource performance across live projects * Support scheduling and reporting requirements for tenders and live contracts * Work closely with project teams to ensure accurate planning and programme alignment * Build strong working relationships with project stakeholders, supply chain partners, and clients * Carry out short\- to medium\-term planning reviews and provide forward\-looking programme analysis * Identify risks and propose mitigation strategies to support project delivery * Review and challenge construction methodologies to improve efficiency and outcomes * Support continuous improvement across planning processes and project execution **Requirements:** * Relevant third\-level qualification (construction, engineering, or related discipline) * Minimum 4\+ years’ experience in a planning role within construction or engineering * Proven experience working on data centre or similar complex projects * Strong proficiency in project planning tools (Primavera P6 essential) * Advanced IT skills, including Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint * Strong analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills * Ability to work collaboratively within multidisciplinary project teams **Package (Typical Benefits):** * Company vehicle or travel allowance * Laptop and mobile phone * Pension scheme * Life insurance and income protection * Ongoing professional development * Lifestyle benefits including wellbeing, sports \& social, cycle to work scheme, and employee assistance programmes

title: QC Analyst ( 12 Months Agency Contract )
description: Rottapharm Limited Viatris is a global healthcare company uniquely positioned to bridge the traditional divide between generics and brands, combining the best of both to more holistically address healthcare needs globally. With a mission to empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life, we provide access at scale, currently supplying high\-quality medicines to approximately 1 billion patients around the world annually and touching all of life's moments, from birth to the end of life, acute conditions to chronic diseases. We have been included on number of award lists that demonstrate the impact we are making. Our global portfolio includes best\-in\-class, iconic brand\-name products as well as global key brands; generics, including branded and complex generics; and other offerings across a wide range of therapeutic areas. We are committed to helping create healthier communities worldwide through education, outreach and better access to treatment. For those driven to live their lives with purpose, Viatris helps employees realise their full potential by creating a collaborative culture that fosters courage and resilience. Viatris is more than just a place to work. This is a place to make a difference in the world. **The Role \& What You Will Be Doing** Every day, we rise to the challenge to make a difference and here’s how the **QC Analyst** role will make an impact: * Testing of raw materials, intermediate and finished product ensuring that all are testing and data review is carried out within the required timelines and in compliance with guidelines * Drive the right first time and laboratory KPIs to ensure they are achieved * Perform as required documentation updates for QC test methods and procedures * Investigate / Trouble shooting of methods * Assist with improvement projects * Maintain proper care in use and maintenance of equipment * Demonstrate ability of analytical techniques and scientific programs (e.g., LIMS/LabX/UV software/Empower) * Assist with laboratory 6S and maintain good housekeeping * Demonstrate ability in scientific report writing e.g., LIR, Protocols, Reports * Analytical data review * Assist with project management * Perform training when required * No supervisory responsibility **About Your Skills \& Experience** For this role, we’re looking for a candidate who has an effective combination of the following qualifications, skills and experiences: * Associate's degree/college diploma in relevant discipline * Experience of a least one year in a Scientist role. HPLC experience advantageous * Self\-motivated and able to prioritise. At Viatris, we are dedicated to building a truly diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you’re excited about this role but your past experience doesn’t fully align with every requirement, we still encourage you to apply. You may just be the right candidate for this or other roles. **Diversity \& Inclusion at Viatris** At Viatris, diversity and inclusion are essential to our mission. The diversity we foster in all aspects of our business can be one of our greatest strengths in redefining healthcare not as it is, but as it should be. If you would like to know more about what diversity, equity and inclusion means to us, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/Careers/Diversity\-and\-Inclusion **Sustainability at Viatris** Corporate social responsibility is fundamental to the Viatris mission. We work to advance responsible and sustainable operations and leverage our collective expertise to empower people to live healthier at every stage of life, recognizing that our actions affect the stakeholders and communities we serve. To learn more about our efforts, please visit https://www.viatris.com/en/about\-us/corporate\-responsibility At Viatris, we offer competitive salaries, benefits and an inclusive environment where you can use your experiences, perspectives and skills to help make an impact on the lives of others. Viatris is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
